sourceTitle: Did You Know LeBron Shoots With the Wrong Hand?

keyPoses.notes: Tall base, ball ready, arms relaxed but asymmetrical.
Compact dip, knees load, left guide hand visibly involved.
Ball rises quickly to forehead area; right elbow tucks and climbs.
High set point, slight rearward fade, shoulders start turning.
Right-hand release, left guide hand peels away, wrist snaps into gooseneck.
Relaxed high follow-through, left arm drops away, soft leg kick.
Feet return under hips; torso settles without over-rotating.

visualPriorities: The release frame must line up with ball spawn and shot-meter ideal release.
The left guide hand peel should be readable because the reference emphasizes LeBron's cross-dominance.
The high right-hand set and relaxed wrist follow-through matter more than exact lower-body mocap.
Foot sliding should be minimized in Blender/manual cleanup later.
